questions.question: Find the GCF of $12$ and $15$ using factors.
A bookshelf has $18$ shelves, and each shelf can hold $12$ books. What is the LCM of $18$ and $12$?
Find the LCM of $8$ and $10$ using prime factorization.
A music store sells guitar strings in packs of $6$ or $8$. What is the GCF of $6$ and $8$?

A group of friends want to go on a road trip that is $240$ miles long. If they drive $15$ miles per hour, how many hours will it take them to complete the trip, assuming they drive for $8$ hours a day? Use GCF and LCM to solve.
Find the GCF of $24$ and $30$ using prime factorization.
A bakery sells bread in loaves of $12$ or $15$. What is the LCM of $12$ and $15$?
A water tank can hold $120$ gallons of water. If $8$ gallons of water are added to the tank every hour, how many hours will it take to fill the tank? Use GCF and LCM to solve.

A group of friends want to go on a camping trip that is $180$ miles long. If they drive $12$ miles per hour, how many hours will it take them to complete the trip, assuming they drive for $8$ hours a day? Use GCF and LCM to solve. Show all work and explain your answer.

A store sells T-shirts in packs of $9$ or $12$. If a customer wants to buy $36$ T-shirts, how many packs of each size should they buy to minimize the total number of packs? Use GCF and LCM to solve. Show all work and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of prime factorization in finding GCF and LCM.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate the concepts of GCF and LCM.
Tip 3: Remind students to show all work and explain their answers clearly.
